source_detection.rule: Treat filenames containing ChatHub or Chat Hub, case-insensitively, as ChatHub multi-LLM source candidates.
source_detection.raw_preservation: Store exact source bytes under SHA-256 before parsing.
source_detection.wording_rule: Do not alter original uploaded framework or model-response wording. Store normalized requirements and any synthesis as separate derived records with source pointers.

paid_subscription.recommended_use: ChatHub browser/web user interface for parallel model questioning and manual or supported export workflows.
paid_subscription.credential_rule: Do not store browser session cookies or scrape authenticated sessions.

custom_chatbot.recommended_use: Expose an approved Jarvis OpenAI-compatible endpoint to ChatHub Custom Chatbot where ChatHub configuration supports it.
custom_chatbot.gate: Requires a scoped endpoint, authentication design, rate limits, privacy review and sandbox proof.

server_api.rule: Do not infer that a paid ChatHub subscription includes a server-to-server API.

upload_and_export.limitations: Unknown or changed export schemas are preserved raw and marked for parser review rather than discarded.

multi_llm_consolidation.preservation: Each response remains verbatim with model identity and source coordinates.
multi_llm_consolidation.derived_output: One consolidated answer may be generated only as a separate artifact.
multi_llm_consolidation.truth_rule: Consensus is not proof; source and claim verification remain required.

security_and_legal_controls.policy: Raw text is preserved exactly, but execution remains subject to applicable law, platform terms, security controls and owner approvals.

open_loops: Validate the current ChatHub export formats against owner-supplied exports.
Validate ChatHub Custom Chatbot interoperability with a scoped Jarvis staging endpoint.
Map all accessible files with ChatHub in their filename into the measured source denominator.
Connect parsed requirements to the persistent Knowledge Fabric and progress tracker.
